records.subject: applicant
records.action: determine whether tree work needs an Urban Forestry permit before work begins
records.object: regulated tree pruning, planting, removal, or related tree work
conditions: Project involves trees regulated by City of Portland Urban Forestry guidance.
Applicant must use public Urban Forestry permit guidance to identify the applicable permit path.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Ask only for non-private project facts needed to identify the applicable Urban Forestry permit path.

source_refs.title: Tree permits
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public Urban Forestry guidance describes tree permit paths and directs applicants to determine whether planned tree work requires a permit.

records.subject: applicant
records.action: check whether Urban Forestry review is part of a broader development or building permit workflow
records.object: tree preservation, planting, or removal review associated with development activity
conditions: Some Urban Forestry reviews are connected to development permit review rather than a standalone tree permit path.
The applicant should identify whether the work is independent tree work or tied to a development proposal.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Route the user to a human or ask clarifying facts when standalone tree-permit guidance and development-review guidance could both apply.

source_refs.title: Trees and development
source_refs.evidence_summary: Urban Forestry public guidance distinguishes tree requirements connected to development review from other tree permit paths.

records.subject: applicant
records.action: prepare and attach required public-form documents and supporting materials through the applicable permit channel
records.object: application materials, tree information, site information, and supporting documentation
conditions: Required materials depend on the Urban Forestry permit path selected.
The fixture records only expected categories of materials and does not include user files.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Produce a checklist of missing document categories without storing uploaded files or private field values.

source_refs.title: Private property tree permits
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public Urban Forestry guidance identifies application paths and supporting information for private-property tree permit requests.

records.subject: agent
records.action: stop before initiating fee payment or entering payment details
records.object: Urban Forestry permit fees or related PP&D/DevHub charges
conditions: Fees may apply depending on the selected permit path and review outcome.
Payment is a financial action and requires exact user confirmation in the active session.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Summarize the fee checkpoint and request explicit confirmation before any payment-related action.

source_refs.title: Tree permit fees
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public Urban Forestry fee guidance describes fee checkpoints for tree permit activity.

source_refs.title: DevHub FAQ
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public DevHub FAQ identifies fee payment as a supported online permitting service category.

records.subject: applicant
records.action: respond to reviewer requests for corrected or additional Urban Forestry materials
records.object: review comments, checksheets, or corrected supporting materials
conditions: Corrections may be required after staff review.
Official correction upload or response is consequential and must be confirmed before action.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Prepare a redacted correction-response preview and stop before official upload or certification.

source_refs.title: DevHub FAQ
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public DevHub FAQ lists correction uploads among online permitting service categories.

records.subject: applicant
records.action: complete required follow-up steps before relying on the Urban Forestry permit as final
records.object: approval conditions, planting obligations, inspections, or closeout instructions
conditions: Some Urban Forestry outcomes include conditions or follow-up steps.
Finalization depends on the specific permit path and approval terms.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Ask for the public approval-condition category or route to PP&D when closeout requirements are unclear.

source_refs.title: Tree permits
source_refs.evidence_summary: Public Urban Forestry permit guidance describes permit outcomes and follow-up responsibilities by permit type.

records.subject: agent
records.action: require exact user confirmation before consequential or financial Urban Forestry permit actions
records.object: submission, official upload, certification, cancellation, inspection request, or payment
conditions: The agent may draft summaries and previews using redacted fixtures.
The agent must not submit, certify, cancel, schedule, upload officially, or pay without exact session-specific confirmation.
records.expected_agent_behavior: Refuse automation of consequential or financial actions until the user explicitly authorizes the exact action in the active session.

source_refs.title: PP&D guarded action executor plan
source_refs.evidence_summary: The PP&D plan requires exact confirmation for consequential and financial actions and forbids unattended irreversible automation.
